title: Four App Router routes: Stitch layout, stub auth, dashboard todos, feed, dark task creation
description: frontend-agent implements four routes 1:1 with Stitch/ui_spec: Dashboard (todo workspace aligned to Stitch, integrating existing todo Server Actions from 05 and organisms from 06 where appropriate), Social Login (stub auth only), Social Activity Feed (client/server fetch of 11 Route Handler JSON), Task Creation (Dark) with route-scoped dark theme segment. Compose Atomic Design components per exports; CSS Modules + tokens from design_system/ui_spec; no Material defaults. EM must set stitch_handoff from design_package after exports.
acceptance: Four App Router routes exist and match paths/names documented in 10-ui-refiner-stitch-ui-spec ui_spec; each route renders layout/section order traceable to canonical Stitch screen stitch.screen_ids[0] and ui_spec (no unconstrained new IA).
Stub login flow satisfies acceptance: user can reach post-stub shell/dashboard without real OAuth; behavior documented for QA E2E.
Dashboard todo workspace uses existing todo domain/actions (Server Actions from 05); CRUD UX remains intact with Stitch-aligned presentation.
Activity feed route consumes the 11-backend-activity-feed-fixture-route handler URL (relative fetch or documented base URL); loading, non-empty, and empty states per ui_spec.
Task Creation (Dark) route applies dark theme only within that segment (layout or nested layout); other three routes remain light per brief.
Accessibility: semantic structure, labels, keyboard paths for interactive controls on all four surfaces per acceptance in ui_spec/critique.
100% unit test line coverage maintained for any new pure UI logic or helpers in declared unit scope; follow repo coverage config and brief exclusions.
implementation_notes: Routes: /login, /dashboard, /activity-feed, /tasks/new; AppShell nav; middleware protects main routes; dark segment layout under /tasks/new.
